items.title: Cats kept in plastic enclosures at Kuala Lumpur market draw online criticism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A video showing cats allegedly being kept in transparent plastic enclosures under the sun at a weekend market in Danau Kota has sparked animal welfare concerns online. The footage, shared on Threads by user marandherhedgehog, appeared to show several cats inside enclosed display boxes at a breeder's stall at the market yesterday. "The cats were left under the hot sun in enclosed containers.

items.title: Stay humble, work hard, Asyraf tells BN after landslide Johor win
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional must work hard and remain humble after securing a landslide victory in the Johor election, says Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. Asyraf said BN will continue to repay the trust given by the people with hard work and dedicated service. "We must work harder for the people.

items.title: PAS respects Johor result, vows to strengthen fight ahead of GE16
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — PAS respects the choice and mandate of the Johor electorate in determining the state’s leadership, says Johor PAS commissioner Datuk Mahfodz Mohamed. Meanwhile, Bersatu secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali said the party would conduct a detailed review of the Johor election results to identify strategic measures to strengthen its political efforts. The 16th Johor State Election, held today, saw BN secure a simple majority after winning 29 of the 56 state seats as of 10.32 pm.

items.title: No seats, no deposits: Bersama suffers crushing Johor election debut
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), making its electoral debut, suffered a major setback after all 15 of its candidates lost their election deposits in the 16th Johor state election today. The constituencies were Bukit Naning, Mahkota, Tiram, Permas, Larkin, Kempas, Kota Iskandar, Bukit Permai, Johor Jaya, Stulang, Perling, Skudai, Senai, Bukit Batu and Puteri Wangsa. In the 16th Johor state election, Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a fresh mandate to continue leading the Johor state government after winning 48 of the 56 state seats contested.

items.title: Perikatan wiped out in Johor as coalition loses all seats, including three incumbencies
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) suffered a crushing defeat in the 16th Johor State Election after failing to win any of the 33 state seats it contested. According to the official results published on the Election Commission (EC) website tonight, PN not only failed to expand its representation but also lost the three seats it won in the 2022 Johor state election, namely Bukit Kepong, Maharani and Endau. In Endau, PN candidate Hasnul Hakimi Hussein failed to wrest the seat from Alwiyah Talib, who represented BN in this election.

items.title: CENTCOM: US launches fresh round of strikes on Iran after attack on cargo ship
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US military said Saturday that it launched a third round of strikes against Iran this week after Iranian forces opened fire on a ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz and announced the waterway's closure, Anadolu Ajansi reported. "US Central Command forces began launching the third round of strikes this week against Iran after Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces blatantly attacked M/V GFS Galaxy," CENTCOM said in a statement on US social media platform X, noting that the strikes began at 7.15pm eastern time (1115GMT). One civilian crew member is missing, the statement said, adding that the vessel was currently "unable to continue the journey due to an onboard fire and significant engine room damage."

items.title: Anwar congratulates BN after Johor victory, pledges federal support for state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 12 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today congratulated Barisan Nasional (BN) on retaining its mandate to form the state government following the 16th Johor state election. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the mandate entrusted to BN was a responsibility that must be carried out with integrity and in the best interests of the state and the people of Johor. Anwar, who is Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, also expressed his appreciation to voters and the people of Johor for fulfilling their democratic responsibility, and thanked election workers and party machinery for their dedication and efforts throughout the campaign period.

items.title: Almost 1,000 evacuated after floods hit Melaka
items.summary: He said Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Tanjung Minyak 2 currently houses 716 victims from 227 families, followed by SK Taman Bukit Rambai with 186 victims from 55 families. JAPERUN Pantai Kundor accommodates 43 victims from 17 families, while SJKC Bertam Ulu houses 24 victims from nine families. The affected locations include Kampung Batu Berendam, Taman Rambai Jaya, Taman Rambai Utama, Kampung Tanjung Minyak, Kampung Bertam Ulu, Kampung Batu Hampar and Rambai Indah.

items.title: Johor poll defeat sends clear message for DAP to improve, says Teo
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching said the party's defeat in the 2026 Johor state election carries a clear message that the party must improve. "This result certainly sends a message for us to do better," she said in a statement last night. With humility, DAP accepts this result and respects the choice of the people of Johor," she said, congratulating BN on its victory.

items.title: EC satisfied with smooth Johor election, says no untoward incidents reported
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 12 — The Election Commission (EC) has expressed satisfaction with the overall conduct of the 16th Johor state election, saying polling was carried out smoothly without any untoward incidents that could have affected the electoral process. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said registered voters had been able to exercise their democratic rights and responsibilities during polling yesterday. Ramlan said voter turnout stood at 68.73 per cent, with 1,874,918 electors comprising early, postal and ordinary voters casting their ballots.

items.title: Early GE16 unlikely after BN's big win in Johor, says Tuan Ibrahim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's administration is unlikely to call an early general election (GE16) following Barisan Nasional's big win in the Johor state polls, says Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man. On the polls, Tuan Ibrahim said the people would be eager to see if Nga Kor Ming would resign as housing and local government minister in line with his remarks on the campaign trail. "For Pas and PN, we must reflect on the results," Tuan Ibrahim said.

items.title: UK police arrest man on suspicion of killing former minister Widdecombe
items.summary: HAYTOR, England: British police said on Saturday that a 28-year-old man has been arrested in South Yorkshire on suspicion of murdering former British government minister Ann Widdecombe. The suspect is a white British national and is now in police custody, the statement said. The man who was released, a white British national, was arrested in Newton Abbot, a town about 15km from Widdecombe's home in Haytor in rural southwest England, police said on Friday.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran after Hormuz ship attack
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US military said on Saturday it launched a new round of strikes against Iran after Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces attacked a Cyprus-flagged container ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz. "A civilian crew member is missing and the vessel is unable to continue the journey due to an onboard fire and significant engineroom damage," the US Central Command said in a statement on X. The US Central Command said the strikes were carried out at the direction of President Donald Trump.--REUTES © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Typhoon Bavi slams into China after nearly 2 million flee
items.summary: BEIJING: Typhoon Bavi made landfall late Saturday in eastern China, packing strong winds as it roared ashore after authorities evacuated nearly two million people, state media said. Typhoon Bavi first made landfall at around 11.20pm Saturday (1520 GMT) in Zhejiang province, the state Xinhua news agency said, quoting the provincial meteorological observatory. As of Saturday morning, authorities had evacuated 1.72 million people to safe places, Xinhua said.

items.title: Time for motorcycle convoy safety laws, says road safety expert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia needs dedicated safety rules for motorcycle convoys, a road safety expert said. Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) Road Safety Research Centre head Associate Professor Dr Law Teik Hua said existing laws under the Road Transport Act did not contain specific provisions governing the size, formation or activities of motorcycle convoys on public roads and expressways. "Regulators could develop official safety guidelines for large-scale motorcycle convoys recommending the maximum size of the convoy, breaking it into smaller sub-convoys, minimum distances between riders and the use of convoy marshals."

items.title: EC hails smooth, incident-free Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Election Commission (EC) expressed satisfaction with the overall conduct of the 16th Johor State Election, saying polling was carried out smoothly without incidents that disrupted the process. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said voters were able to exercise their democratic rights during polling yesterday. Ramlan said the EC would continue reviewing the initiative should issues arise.

items.title: PN wiped out in Johor after losing all 33 seats
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) suffered a crushing defeat in the 16th Johor State Election after failing to win any of the 33 seats it contested. BN successfully retained the Johor State Government after winning 48 seats, while PH secured eight. Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), MUDA, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia, Parti Sosialis Malaysia and independents failed to win any seats.

items.title: Johor SMEs want bigger role in state's investment boom
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Small and medium enterprises (SMEs) in Johor hope for tangible opportunities arising from investments in the state, particularly in terms of financing and market access. Suraya said the next state government should ensure investment projects created stronger linkages with local businesses through structured vendor development programmes. She said major investors and multinational companies entering Johor should be encouraged to source goods and services from local businesses.

items.title: Johor needs data-driven housing policy, says property expert
items.summary: KLUANG: The next Johor government must use data to guide its affordable housing policy and ensure project approvals are swift and efficient. Tan said the Johor Economic Transformation Plan could ensure more balanced development by strengthening economic clusters in different districts. Approval speed is important in housing development," he said.

items.title: Penang pushes for excellence in specialised cardiac care
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow on Thursday visited Northern Heart Hospital Penang, reaffirming the Penang government's commitment to advancing quality healthcare in the state. Hospital management said the visit provided an opportunity to showcase its clinical capabilities, dedicated healthcare professionals and vision of contributing to Penang's healthcare ecosystem. Northern Heart Hospital Penang said it remains committed to delivering world-class cardiovascular care through clinical excellence, innovation, compassion and patient-centred services.

items.title: Iran's IRGC navy says Strait of Hormuz closed until further notice, state media reports
items.summary: IRAN: Iran's Revolutionary Guards navy said early on Sunday it had closed the Strait of Hormuz until further notice after firing a warning shot at a vessel it said had attempted to transit along an unapproved route, state media reported. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Firefighters gain upper hand on deadly Spain wildfire
items.summary: SPAIN: Firefighters battling a wildfire in southern Spain that killed 12 people started to contain the blaze on Saturday as the hardest-hit village remained deserted, with charred vegetation and blackened homes a grim reminder of the inferno that sent terrified people fleeing from their homes. Around 500 firefighters backed by over 20 water-dropping aircraft were battling the blaze which erupted Thursday in the Gallardos area of the southern region of Andalusia, home to many foreign residents. Calmer winds and higher air humidity levels allowed firefighters to directly attack the flames for the first time, officials said.

items.title: Plans for post-war Gaza face challenges on the ground
items.summary: Plans for governance, security and humanitarian relief have begun to take shape, but remain largely theoretical in the absence of a political agreement, credible security guarantees and sustained funding. SECURITY CONCERNS Security remains the cornerstone of every post-war scenario under discussion. Israel insists Hamas must disarm before any progress can be made, while the Palestinian Islamist movement refuses to surrender its weapons before Israeli troops withdraw and a Palestinian governing authority is established.

items.title: US seeks free Hormuz access from Iran as talks focus on strait
items.summary: DUBAI: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi and his Omani counterpart on Saturday discussed arrangements for the safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz, as Washington seeks a public pledge of free, secure transit. US President Donald Trump said on Friday the US and Iran had agreed to continue talks despite an escalation of hostilities this week, while also declaring an end to the ceasefire between the two countries. Oman's state news agency later said that Omani and Iranian negotiators would continue talks "at the technical and political levels."

items.title: Trump revives praise for his health, cognitive test in post about May physical
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he had "just finished" a physical at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center, referring to the medical examination he underwent in late May after which the White House said he was in excellent health. The White House said Trump was referring to his May physical. Haberman and Swan's "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump" described concerns among some White House aides about Trump's age, stamina and physical condition.

items.title: Paris landmarks shutter early as France bakes in latest heatwave
items.summary: FRANCE: The Eiffel Tower and other Paris landmarks closed early over the weekend and cycling's Tour de France shortened a stage for the first time in its history, as France sweated through its third heatwave since May. As of Saturday, a quarter of mainland France was sweltering under the country's highest heat alert level, including the Paris region. The operator of the Eiffel Tower said the latticed-steel monument would "exceptionally close" early on Saturday and Sunday at 4pm (1400 GMT), "due to the high temperatures forecast."

items.title: Israel police arrest 4 settlers over West Bank attack on CNN crew
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israeli police said they arrested four suspects on Saturday for allegedly damaging a vehicle carrying foreign journalists in the occupied West Bank, in an incident that US broadcaster CNN described as an attack on its crew by Israeli settlers. CNN said the group later climbed onto another vehicle carrying journalists that was travelling behind its team and smashed its windshield. In a statement, Israeli police said they received a report alleging that suspects had blocked a vehicle carrying several foreign journalists near Sinjil.

items.title: Russian strikes kill seven, wound dozens, Ukraine's Zelenskyy seeks faster weapons deliveries
items.summary: KYIV: Russia launched missile and drone attacks on Ukraine on Saturday, killing seven people and wounding dozens more, officials said, as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for diplomatic efforts to ensure Kyiv secured weapons more quickly. Kyiv awaited supplies of air defence munitions amid a shortage that has left it exposed to Russian attacks. Two glide bombs hit a crowded area in the northern city of Sumy, a frequent Russian target, killing five people and injuring 30, authorities said.

items.title: Trump administration subpoenas New York Times journalists over Air Force One story, newspaper says
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The New York Times said on Saturday that the US Department of Justice had ordered several of its journalists to testify before a federal grand jury after they reported on security concerns involving President Donald Trump's new Qatari-donated Air Force One. The subpoenas were issued by Jay Clayton, the US attorney in Manhattan, and in some cases delivered to reporters' homes by federal agents, the news outlet reported. The National Press Club urged the Justice Department to "immediately" withdraw the subpoenas.

items.title: [OFFICIAL] BN wins 48 seats to form next Johor government
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Election Commission today confirmed that Barisan Nasional has secured more than a two-thirds majority in the 16th Johor election. Its chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said that as of 12.50am, BN had won 48 of the 56 seats, an increase of eight seats compared to the 2022 state election. He added that the remaining eight seats went to Pakatan Harapan.

items.title: Iran supreme leader vows revenge for father's killing
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's supreme leader on Saturday vowed revenge for the US-Israeli killing of his father and predecessor, hours after President Donald Trump warned against any attempt to assassinate him. He became Iran's supreme leader after his father Ali Khamenei's killing in late February by US-Israeli strikes, but has not been in public since before the war. He said Iran had compiled a list of individuals to be targeted.

items.title: Typhoon Bavi makes landfall in eastern China after nearly 2 million evacuated
items.summary: WENZHOU: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in the coastal city of Taizhou in eastern China late on Saturday, after nearly two million people were evacuated as the storm swept through the region. The typhoon had earlier battered Japan's southern Sakishima island chain and passed near northern Taiwan, bringing heavy rain and strong winds. Taiwan's fire department said 113 people were injured, mostly due to falls from motorcycles or bicycles, falls, or being struck by objects.

items.title: [OFFICIAL] Full results of the 16th Johor state election
items.summary: Noraziah Mohd Razit (PH-PKR) - 5,454 Winner: Datuk Zahari Sarip (BN-UMNO) – Majority: 8,600 2022: Zahari Sarip (BN-UMNO) – Majority: 5,377 N02 Jementah 1. Ng Kor Sim (PH-DAP) - 11,609 Winner: See Ann Giap (BN-MCA) - 12,522 Majority: 913 2022: Ng Kor Sim (PH-DAP) - Majority: 714 N03 Pemanis 1. Uzzair Ismail (PN-Bersatu) - 3,545 Winner: K. Raven Kumar (BN-MIC) – Majority: 3,838 2022: N. Saraswati (BN-MIC) – Majority: 1,611 N05 Tenang 1.

items.title: MCA overtakes DAP in Johor polls, wins eight seats in major BN boost
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: MCA has secured eight seats in the Johor state assembly, surpassing DAP's six-seat tally in a significant breakthrough for Barisan Nasional's (BN) Chinese-based component party. The victory could also ease longstanding calls for MCA to reconsider its position within BN following years of electoral setbacks. Meanwhile, DAP saw its representation in the state assembly fall from 10 seats to six, losing three seats to MCA and one to MIC.

items.title: Johor menteri besar to be sworn in today after BN's landslide win
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The appointment and swearing-in of Johor's new menteri besar is expected to take place later today following Barisan Nasional's (BN) commanding victory in the Johor election. BN secured 48 of the 56 state seats, eight more than it won in the 2022 Johor election, giving the coalition a strong mandate to form the next Johor government. The Johor election saw BN secure a commanding majority, while Pakatan Harapan (PH) won eight seats and Perikatan Nasional (PN) failed to retain any of the three seats it won in the 2022 Johor election.

items.title: Pakatan admits Johor defeat after ‘shock’ eight-seat showing
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has admitted defeat in the Johor state election after securing only eight of the 56 seats contested. At a press conference tonight, PH Johor state election director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari acknowledged the coalition’s disappointing performance. Amirudin said PH had secured eight seats based on the EC results available as of 9.50pm, with DAP winning six seats and PKR and Amanah taking one each.

items.title: EC tally shows BN wins landslide Johor victory with 48 seats, Pakatan reduced to eight
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a landslide victory in the 16th Johor state election today, with the latest Election Commission (EC) tally showing the coalition winning 48 of the state’s 56 seats. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun, however, announced that BN had secured at least 29 seats, confirming that the coalition had crossed the simple-majority threshold needed to form the state government. PH suffered heavy losses after winning 12 seats in the 2022 state election, reducing its representation by four seats.

items.title: EC confirms unofficial results give BN simple majority in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Unofficial results announced by the Election Commission (EC) show that Barisan Nasional (BN) has secured a simple majority in the 16th Johor state election. During a Facebook livestream, the EC said BN had won at least 29 of the 56 seats in the Johor Legislative Assembly, giving the coalition enough seats to form the state government. The results remain subject to formal declarations by the respective returning officers following the completion of counting and tabulation.

items.title: ‘We must serve everyone’: Onn Hafiz thanks Johoreans for backing BN
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, July 11 — With unofficial results showing Barisan Nasional (BN) winning enough seats to form the next Johor government with a simple majority, Johor BN chief Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi urged all newly elected representatives to serve the people faithfully. Onn Hafiz, who retained the Machap state seat, said the elected representatives now carried a great responsibility to serve the people, stressing that their mandate was to look after all Johoreans. “Now that we have won, we must serve everyone — Malays, Chinese, Indians and Orang Asli — because we are all Bangsa Johor,” he said at a press conference attended by BN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.

items.title: Unofficial: BN claims landslide Johor election victory
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has claimed an unofficial victory in 49 of the 56 seats contested in today’s Johor state election. Perikatan Nasional (PN) failed to secure any seats despite contesting across the state. Asyraf had earlier posted unofficial results showing BN leading in 33 seats.

items.title: BN top leaders gather at Johor operations room to track election results
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Top Barisan Nasional (BN) leaders began gathering at the coalition’s main operations room at the Johor UMNO Liaison Committee building tonight to monitor developments and the official results of the Johor State Election. Among those present were UMNO president and BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi and BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. Meanwhile, the atmosphere at Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) main operations room at Pulai Springs Resort also saw the presence of Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) vice-president Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad.

items.title: MMEA detains tanker suspected in collision with fishing boat
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 11 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) in Penang has detained a tanker believed to have been involved in a collision with a local fishing vessel in the waters off Bagan Datuk, Perak. Its director, Maritime Captain Muhammad Suffi Mohd Ramli, said the tanker was believed to have continued its voyage to Penang waters after the incident on Thursday. He said following information received from the Perak Maritime Operations Centre (PUSOP), the agency deployed assets to locate and intercept the tanker.

items.title: EC: Pakatan gets first unofficial Johor win in Bentayan
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, July 11 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) has secured its first seat of the night by retaining Bentayan through incumbent Ng Yak Howe, based on unofficial results announced by the Election Commission (EC). The EC disclosed the result during a Facebook livestream as vote counting continued across Johor. BN, meanwhile, has won 13 seats so far — Bukit Permai, Penawar, Kukup, Machap, Sedili, Bekok, Tenggaroh, Benut, Pekan Nanas, Pasir Raja, Yong Peng, Rengit and Bukit Kepong.

items.title: BN takes Machap, Kukup and Sedili in first three unofficial Johor results announced by EC
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, July 11 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has secured the first three seats announced by the Election Commission (EC) in the 16th Johor state election, based on unofficial results. The EC said during a Facebook livestream that BN’s Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi won Machap, while Muzaide Makmor and Md Israk Abdullah took Sedili and Kukup respectively. BN also won all three constituencies in the 2022 state election, with Onn Hafiz and Muzaide retaining their seats, while Md Israk succeeded former Kukup assemblyman Datuk Jefridin Atan as the coalition’s candidate.

items.title: Govt approves over 5,000 Budi Diesel quota transfers within families
items.summary: CAMERON HIGHLANDS, July 11 — The government has approved more than 5,000 applications to transfer subsidised diesel quotas under the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) programme to immediate family members, making it easier for those who actually use eligible vehicles to benefit from the subsidy. “One of the complaints we received was that Budi Diesel is only available to the registered owner of a diesel vehicle. Johan said eligible vehicle owners can apply to transfer their subsidised diesel quota online through the Budi Madani portal at budimadani.gov.my or at any Inland Revenue Board (LHDN) office.

items.title: Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad quits Bersatu
items.summary: TAMPIN, July 11 — Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad has reportedly resigned from Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) and stepped down as the party’s Tampin division chief with immediate effect. According to Sinar Harian , Ridzuan confirmed the contents of a resignation letter dated today that had been circulated publicly. Ridzuan said his departure would allow Bersatu to appoint new leadership that could better lead its Tampin division.

items.title: Live update: EC confirms BN wins 29 Johor seats to form state government
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — [10.50pm] : The Election Commission (EC) has announced that Barisan Nasional (BN) has won 29 out of the 56 state seats contested in the 16th Johor state election. The result gives BN the simple majority needed to form the Johor state government. [8.40pm:] Unofficial results show Barisan Nasional (BN) has won 31 of the 56 Johor state seats, giving the coalition enough seats to form the next state government with a simple majority.

items.title: Johor polls: Turnout reaches 67.44pc by 5pm with over 1.8 million voters casting ballots
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.8 million voters, or 67.44 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 5pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). Sri Medan recorded the highest turnout at 77.2 per cent, followed by Semarang at 76.8 per cent and Sedili at 74.3 per cent. Bekok had the lowest turnout at 58.9 per cent, followed by Stulang at 60.5 per cent and Perling at 60.6 per cent.

items.title: Johor polls: Turnout crosses 63pc as more than 1.69 million voters cast ballots before polls close
items.summary: The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. At 3pm, the Semarang state constituency recorded the highest voter turnout at 66.1 per cent, followed by Sri Medan at 64.4 per cent and Penawar at 64.2 per cent. Penggaram recorded the lowest turnout at 48.8 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 49 per cent and Perling at 50.3 per cent.

items.title: Typhoon Bavi forces Malaysia Airlines to cancel Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai flights
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — Malaysia Airlines has announced additional flight cancellations involving the Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai route due to adverse weather conditions associated with Typhoon Bavi, following yesterday’s advisory on cancellations involving the Kuala Lumpur-Taipei route. In a statement today, the national carrier said the affected flights on July 11 are MH386 (Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai), MH388 (Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai) and MH389 (Shanghai-Kuala Lumpur). Yesterday, Malaysia Airlines announced the cancellation of flights MH366 (Kuala Lumpur-Taipei) and MH367 (Taipei-Kuala Lumpur), originally scheduled to operate on July 11, due to Typhoon Bavi.

items.title: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao route opens to ease Malaysia-Thailand border congestion
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM, July 11 — The new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah with the Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex in Thailand was opened to the public today. One motorist, Mohd Faizal Ahmad, 42, from Penang, said the new route was better organised and offered greater convenience for Malaysians travelling to Thailand. We hope the new route will help reduce the problem,” he said.

items.title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 56.77pc by 3pm with more than 1.52 million ballots cast
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.52 million voters, or 56.77 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 3pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. The Johor state election involves more than 2.6 million registered voters who are choosing representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.

items.title: Thunderstorm warning issued for eight states and KL as heavy rain, strong winds expected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to affect eight states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 5 pm today. The warning also covers Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim in Perak. Similar weather conditions are forecast in Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat in Selangor; Jelebu in Negeri Sembilan; and Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang and Mersing in Johor.

items.title: Pulau Besar becomes first Johor polling centre to close after 32 voters cast ballots
items.summary: MERSING, July 11 — Balai Raya Pulau Besar became the first polling centre to close in today’s Johor state election. “The polling centres at Balai Raya Pulau Aur and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Pulau Pemanggil closed at noon, while Balai Raya Pulau Sibu and SK Pulau Tinggi closed at 2 pm,” he told Bernama. All polling centres across the five islands cater to 690 registered voters.

items.title: Former Umno leader Isham Jalil applies to join PAS three years after party sacking
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — Former Umno Supreme Council member Isham Jalil has applied to join PAS, nearly three years after he was sacked from the party. Isham was sacked from Umno in December 2023, months after he was elected to the party’s Supreme Council during its internal elections. Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki had previously said Isham was removed for allegedly being disloyal, failing to follow party orders and directives, and not respecting party decisions.

items.title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 48.91pc by 2pm with more than 1.31 million ballots cast
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.31 million voters, or 48.91 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 2pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state. The Johor state election involves more than 2.6 million registered voters who are choosing representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.

items.title: AirBorneo to open ticket sales for Kuching-Singapore flights ahead of July 22 launch
items.summary: KUCHING, July 11 — AirBorneo Airways will begin selling tickets for its inaugural international service between Kuching and Singapore on July 13, ahead of the launch of daily flights on July 22, which coincides with the Hari Sarawak celebration. “The new daily service between Kuching International Airport and Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4 will strengthen Sarawak’s connectivity with one of the region’s key aviation and business hubs, while opening new opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and Singapore,” the statement said. He said the Kuching-Singapore service marked a significant step in AirBorneo’s growth, extending Sarawak’s air connectivity beyond Malaysia’s borders for the first time.

items.title: Sarawak Immigration crackdown around Kuching, Kota Samarahan nets 24 foreign nationals
items.summary: KUCHING, July 11 — The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 24 foreign nationals during Op Minyak, Op Selera, and Op Mahir, which were carried out at seven locations around Kuching and Kota Samarahan yesterday. Inspections focused on heavy machinery workshops in Kampung Semeba and Kampung Sudat in Kuching, as well as eateries and premises suspected of conducting online gambling activities in Muara Tuang, Kota Samarahan. Several scrap metal and second-hand goods premises around Kota Samarahan were also inspected to ensure compliance with the country’s immigration laws.

items.title: LPT crash: More than 30 motorcycles believed involved in illegal racing, says police
items.summary: District police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said 18 victims were treated at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA), while two others received treatment at Sultan Ahmad Shah Medical Centre here. Earlier, Ashari said the crash, which occurred at about 1 am, claimed the lives of Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, who were all riding Yamaha RXZ motorcycles. He said another victim, Muhammad Safawi Md Noh, 24, died while receiving treatment at HTAA.

items.title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 42pc by 1pm, ahead of previous state election pace
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.13 million voters, or 42.16 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 1pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC). In a Facebook post, the EC said the Semarang and Sedili state constituencies recorded the highest voter turnout at 50.5 per cent each, followed by Tanjung Surat at 48.6 per cent. Penggaram registered the lowest turnout at 32.2 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 32.6 per cent and Simpang Jeram at 35.2 per cent.
